title: Incorrect LIKE result
description:
drh added on 2021-02-15 16:51:12:
The following SELECT statement returns 0, or does a branch on an uninitialized
value, when it should be returning 1:
    SELECT 'x' LIKE '%' ESCAPE '_';

The probably apparently only arises when the "ESCAPE '_'" clause
is used.  Found by OSSFuzz.  Bisect shows that the problem was introduced
by check-in [11e0844f71e8f2d2].
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, name String, value Float64, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now());
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('test_x', NULL);
SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE '%';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL OR name LIKE '%';
key_bug_features: LIKE clause with ESCAPE '_' returns incorrect result
